records.name: home
records.title: UKMTO
records.text_excerpt: UKMTO UKMTO Operations centre Who We Are? About us Trusted. Connected. Ready. For more than 25 years, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) Centre has been a trusted authority in the maritime security sector, helping protect crucial trade routes by providing mariners, shipping companies, and regional authorities with essential , verified and corroborated security information. UKMTO’s approach is and will always be, to support any mariner and maritime trade customer in the provision of information and reassurance. I n the Middle East and Indian Ocean Region, through the Single Information Framework (SIF) agreement, UKMTO collaborates with global maritime stakeholders to provide timely and validated security alerts. UKMTO operates a Voluntary Reporting Scheme (VRS) within the Voluntary Reporting Area (VRA) outlined in UKHO Maritime Security Chart Q6099. UKMTO acts as the primary point of contact for emergency response. About us reporting Report an incident UKMTO 24/7 +44 (0) 2392 222060 Genera

records.name: warnings
records.title: Warnings
records.text_excerpt: Warnings Warnings UKMTO produces a product termed as Warning. These warnings include Attack, Boarding, Hijack and other Maritime Security related incidents. These Warnings are published and sent to Industry in order to provide warning to Masters and CSO’s that an incident is taking place within the UKMTO Voluntary Report Area (VRA). Warnings Product list Home About us Recent Incidents UKMTO Products Partner Products Reporting Formats Best Practices Contact us Email: [email protected] Phone: +44 (0) 2392 222065 Emergency Email: [email protected] Emergency Phone: +44 (0) 2392 222060 © Crown copyright Terms & Conditions

records.name: advisories
records.title: Advisories
records.text_excerpt: Advisories Advisories UKMTO produces a product termed an Advisory. An Advisory is a security related report received from a vessel within the Voluntary Reporting Area (VRA) which do not meet the BMP criteria for raising a report. These reports of suspicious or unusual activity, or patterns of behaviour within the VRA are received from any source including a ships master, a member of the crew, embarked Private Security Team (PST), a vessel’s Company Security Officer or a Security Company. Advisories Product List Home About us Recent Incidents UKMTO Products Partner Products Reporting Formats Best Practices Contact us Email: [email protected] Phone: +44 (0) 2392 222065 Emergency Email: [email protected] Emergency Phone: +44 (0) 2392 222060 © Crown copyright Terms & Conditions
